reasoning: The market prediction of 5% in mid-April makes logical sense. As of late April 2025, Google's Gemini 2.5 Pro Experimental strongly leads the main LMArena leaderboard, with OpenAI's o3 and Meta's Llama 4 Maverick (briefly) taking the other top spots. DeepSeek's V3 and R1 successfully contended for the top 3 in early 2025, but have since fallen slightly behind these massive new frontier models. DeepSeek is expected to release its R2 and V4 models around May 2025. To win this market, DeepSeek would either need these models to hold the #1 spot for over seven months against upcoming releases of GPT-5, Claude 4, and Gemini updates, or drop another frontier model right before the end of the year. Given DeepSeek's smaller team size (160 people) and the sheer capital scaling advantages of competitors like OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic, it is highly improbable for DeepSeek to be #1 exactly on December 31, 2025. I assign a 4% probability to account for their excellent engineering track record and favorable tie-breaking alphabetical rules against Google, Meta, and OpenAI.
